mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market slack signals severe demand contraction and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a bivariate regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect immediate supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output expansion, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of rising INDPRO within late-cycle expansions, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth rates directly forecast corporate earnings revisions, establishing a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, negative real yields trigger stagflation fears and force equity deleveraging, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The specified inflation-rate regime yields positive 60-day returns in > 60% of historical occurrences, or the predictive coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which immediately boosts equity risk appetite and generates positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined payroll-unemployment signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with a joint model ICIR below 0.3 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising industrial production alongside accelerating CPI, supply bottlenecks compress corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently, or the mechanism's predictive accuracy drops below random chance in supply-constrained regimes.
